channel.title: Maintenance on Jsonnet-as-a-Service
channel.description: Recent content in Maintenance on Jsonnet-as-a-Service

item.title: Operations
item.description:
Day-two operations for a running JaaS install. Initial install and hardening decisions are in Kubernetes and Production .

Graceful shutdown and drain

When Kubernetes sends SIGTERM, JaaS executes a two-phase shutdown to avoid dropping in-flight requests:

  1. The readiness probe flips to false (503 on /ready). Kubernetes endpoint controllers begin deregistering the pod from Services.
  2. JaaS waits for --shutdown-delay (default 5s) before closing its listeners. This window lets the endpoint propagation complete so no new traffic arrives after the server closes.
  3. After the delay, the servers shut down gracefully with a 30-second context.WithTimeout. The operator goroutine is also cancelled and awaited within the same 30-second window.

The distroless runtime image has no sleep binary, so the drain delay is implemented in the binary rather than via a preStop hook. A second SIGTERM (or SIGINT) during the drain cuts the wait short.
